localBiteReport: The most current angler reports from the Choctawhatchee Bay area (within the past week, sourced from Captain Experiences) show speckled trout running well near Santa Rosa Beach, with multiple fish landed including some quality specimens. Bull redfish are also active — one notable catch logged six days ago was a 40-inch, 26-pound bull red. A guide out of the Destin/Shalimar area noted that the inshore bite had been tough due to persistent winds but was improving daily as conditions settled, with bay water temps sitting in the low 80s keeping trout scattered but hungry. Flounder were also reported showing up on bay trips alongside reds and trout. No specific sheepshead or black drum reports surfaced this week, though both are seasonally present on structure.
localBiteSource: Captain Experiences (captainexperiences.com) – Choctawhatchee Bay & Destin fishing reports, July 2026; FishingBooker (fishingbooker.com) – Choctawhatchee Bay reports · Auto-refreshed via Claude API
